items.title: Melbourne woman suffers acute liver failure after using knock-off drug

children.content: Megan Hancocks bought a drug labelled as the peptide compound retatrutide in cash from a beautician. Within a week of her second dose, she was in acute liver failure.

gallery.altText: A woman lies in a hospital bed with her eyes closed.

thumbnail.altText: A woman lies in a hospital bed with her eyes closed.

items.title: Steve Irwin 'wouldn't have wanted this': Australia Zoo calls police on traditional owner

children.content: Traditional owner Alwyn Lyall says he felt "extremely upset and humiliated" when police asked him to leave Australia Zoo, where he was trying to see a crocodile he says was "wrongfully arrested" and taken from the wild.

image.altText: A large crocodile sitting near the water, photographed through the trees.

items.title: First case of deadly H5 bird flu confirmed in Australia

children.content: The virus, found in a migratory bird in Esperance, raises concerns for the poultry industry despite Australia having spent years preparing for such a biosecurity breach.

image.altText: a scientist hold up a test tube that says 'bird flu' on it

items.title: A diabolical Iran deal reveals the Trump administration's delusions

children.content: Just as Netanyahu's claims to be able to influence Trump have now become a burden to him, Trump's limited capacity to influence Netanyahu spells just as much trouble for peace in the Middle East.

image.altText: Donald Trump and Benjamin Netanyahu

items.title: Could the 'bliss' trade set millions up for a comfortable retirement?

children.content: Does "bliss" — big lasting state support for financial entities deemed too big to fail — make the stock market a one-way bet?

image.altText: An image of Elon Musk on the New York Stock Exchange tower

items.title: New football laws strike as Almirón sent off

children.content: Paraguay's Miguel Almirón fell victim to new laws put in place at this World Cup to counter discriminatory behaviour. So what are the new laws in place?

articles.title: Cristian Volpato shows why the Socceroos wanted him

children.content: Cristian Volpato only made a 30-minute cameo for the Socceroos in their 2-0 World Cup defeat against the USA, but in doing so showed his quality and desire to excel for Australia.

image.altText: Cristian Volpato runs with the ball

articles.title: Quick hits: NFL flavour at the World Cup and Osito the dog charms fans

children.content: Scotland feels hard done by in their defeat against Morocco and a former NFL legend is on hand to photograph as the son of another of the NFL alumni cohort scores for his country. Here's the quick hits from the FIFA World Cup.

image.altText: QHdog

articles.title: Socceroos receive World Cup wake-up call and have a week to find answers

children.content: Coming into the game on a high, the Socceroos copped a reality check in the first half against the USA. How they respond over the next week will define their 2026 World Cup campaign.

image.altText: Harry Souttar looks confronts Folarin Balogun as other players intervene

gallery.altText: Miguel Almiron argues in the face of the ref

thumbnail.altText: Miguel Almiron argues in the face of the ref

items.title: Transatlantic slave trade gravest crime against humanity, Juneteenth marks its end

children.content: Americans are commemorating Juneteenth months after a UN resolution declaring the transatlantic slave trade the gravest crime against humanity.

image.altText: The side profile of a woman's face. She's wearing Black Lives Matter earrings with a woman at centre, fist raised

items.title: Knight sent off for alleged homophobic slur, Dolphins superstar sidelined

children.content: Newcastle back rower Asu Kepaoa is sent off during a NSW Cup game for an alleged homophobic slur. Meanwhile, in the NRL, Dolphins halfback Isaiya Katoa is missing for the Dolphins' showdown with the Tigers due to a knee injury. Follow live.

image.altText: The chest of a Newcastle Knights player.

items.title: One dead after huge fire at Dominican beach resort

children.content: A woman has been killed and nearly 1,700 tourists evacuated due to a large fire at a hotel in the Dominican beach resort town of Bayahibe.

image.altText: Drone vision of thick pluming smoke and flames over a beachside resort in the Dominican Republic.

gallery.altText: A resort on fire next to bright blue water.

thumbnail.altText: A resort on fire next to bright blue water.

items.title: Frantic finish coming up as Crows, Demons in nailbiter

children.content: The Crows take on the Demons at Adelaide Oval, with a spot in the top four on the line, in the first of three games of Saturday football. Follow live.

image.altText: An Adelaide Crows AFL player leans forward to take a low mark in front of a Melbourne player.

items.title: 'Everything's just so wrong': Inside the country town that turned to One Nation

children.content: In just a few short weeks, a small country town became One Nation heartland. It is a blueprint for how the party can turn Australia orange.

image.altText: Older man wearing blue jumper and orange One nation hat stands with arms outstretched in front of service station

items.title: Twin brothers born deaf hear their mum's voice for first time after epic surgery

children.content: An Australian-first eight hour surgical feat had one main purpose — for Artie and Jack to hear their mum's voice at the same time.

gallery.altText: Twin boys sitting in high chair smiling.
A baby play with toys on the floor.
A mother kisses her baby boy on the cheek.

thumbnail.altText: Twin boys sitting in high chair smiling.
A baby play with toys on the floor.
A mother kisses her baby boy on the cheek.

items.title: No bail for brothers accused of wreaking havoc after Newcastle riot

children.content: A court hears two brothers were tracked riding recklessly for 13km and attempted to evade police following a riot in Newcastle.

image.altText: A man in hand cuffs with his face blurred being ushered into the back of a police van.

items.title: The immersive Indigenous language class where English is left at the door

children.content: After learning from Native American Yuchi families, Pertame elders and young mothers in Alice Springs are raising babies in a total immersion Language Nest and hope to extend the model into primary school.

image.altText: A woman plays blocks with a child.

items.title: Why these retirees won't give up the 3:30am starts and 14-hour shifts

children.content: When the alarm goes off at 3:30am in this freezing outback pit-stop, it's music to the ears of the volunteer bakers who happily spend their winters in a bakery like no other.

gallery.altText: A man in a cap laughing in a kitchen with women laughing behind him.
Two men carry trays of freshly baked bread loaves up steps from an underground baker.

thumbnail.altText: A man in a cap laughing in a kitchen with women laughing behind him.
Two men carry trays of freshly baked bread loaves up steps from an underground baker.

items.title: If you didn't do it at 40, do it at 60

children.content: For some women, the act of asking, "what might still be possible" — of refusing to accept that the most meaningful chapters are already behind them — might be its own kind of resistance.

image.altText: Tilda Swinton poses on a red carpet wearing a red long sleeve dress and gold choker

items.title: Applause for Modra as Ricciuto describes optimism following surgery

children.content: Adelaide Crows fans clap at the sixth minute of the team's match against Melbourne to wish former number-six player Tony Modra well in his recovery from surgery following a serious truck crash.

image.altText: A man wearing a T-shirt with Tony Modra's picture on it under a blue jacket

items.title: Mother holds onto hope her son's killer will be found after remote WA murder

children.content: The grieving mother of Josh Warneke, who was found dead on the side of a road in WA's remote Kimberley, has faced a long battle for answers. But how likely is it the 21-year-old's killer will be brought to justice?

image.altText: Image of a photograph of Josh Warneke placed in front of a memorial in Broome, Western Australia.

items.title: Zombie director Yeon Sang-ho says AI wants to 'fold' us into one

children.content: Train to Busan director Yeon Sang-ho is ringing the warning bell over AI's potential to strip humans of their individuality with his new zombie film Colony.

gallery.altText: Colony director and writer Yeon Sang-ho.

thumbnail.altText: Colony director and writer Yeon Sang-ho.

items.title: Sisters separated in Afghanistan are becoming Australian citizens

children.content: Five years ago, Adiba was separated from her sister Arya as she fled Afghanistan. Now the Ganji siblings have reunited in Melbourne and are on their way to obtaining Australian citizenship.

image.altText: Adiba and Arya, two sisters, holding hands, smiling

items.title: The women planting the seeds of a flower farm movement

children.content: Women looking for a new career or to invest in themselves are turning to micro flower farms, but pricing their blooms against industry giants can be challenging.

items.title: What Off Campus can teach us about centring women's pleasure

children.content: The college hockey romance has had millions of people swooning over its main characters and the messages around healthy relationships.

items.title: What does it mean for a nation to be a 'monoculture'?

children.content: Pauline Hanson has called for Australia to reject multiculturalism and be "monocultural". So what does it involve and are there any nations overseas that show what it might look like in Australia?

items.title: 'He got that wrong': Schwarzer says selection mistakes cost Socceroos

children.content: The Socceroos will rue a missed opportunity after a dire first half against the USA, with Tony Popovic's selections at least partly to blame, says Mark Schwarzer.

image.altText: Tony Popovic looks stern

items.title: Megastars bring new life to 'aging' country music

children.content: The country music genre has grown in popularity since the pandemic, spawning huge hits and an equally huge following in 2026 that nobody saw coming.

items.title: New country choir boosts queer visibility

children.content: A choir formed in the tiny village of Candelo, on the New South Wales Far South Coast, is giving a voice to the LGBTQ+ community.

items.title: Proposed urea plant secures $2.4b worth of deals without being built

children.content: The Australian Fertilizer Corporation has secured offtake agreements with international and domestic customers to sell its entire production capacity, despite not yet being built.

items.title: So much for the promise that Tasmanians deserve better from politicians

children.content: After last year's snap election, Tasmania's premier declared voters were "sick and tired of the political games". After this week, it is clear that the politicians just cannot help themselves.

items.title: Not so long ago, politicians cared about the optics of big donations

children.content: One Nation's dramatic return to the national stage seems to have dashed any sense of restraint or shame about ties to wealthy donors.
